design_philosophy.core_loop: Select anthropomorphic vehicle with distinct physics properties, race through polygonal 3D tracks using boost and tight cornering mechanics, master vehicle-specific handling to optimize lap times
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Vehicle weight and center-of-gravity management vs. track navigation speed; heavier vehicles require more skillful cornering but offer stability tradeoffs
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Pilot a personality-driven race car with distinctive visual character through visually impressive 3D environments; demonstrate mastery through understanding subtle vehicle dynamics
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Game emphasizes realistic vehicle physics and weight simulation over arcade accessibility—heavier vehicles are deliberately clumsier to reflect actual physics dynamics. Shigeru Miyamoto's directive prioritized these subtle details as rewards for mastery rather than simplification. The anthropomorphic vehicles (with eyes as headlights) suggest personality-driven experience rather than pure simulation.

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Vehicle selection (3 standard + 1 unlockable + 1 bonus) provides implicit difficulty scaling—F-Type with rear-heavy center of gravity requires more skill than 4WD monster truck; multiple game modes (racing, time-attack, obstacle courses, test-drive) allow players to practice and develop mastery

progression.unlock_mechanism: 2WD vehicle unlockable through progression; Trailer semi-truck available in bonus games
replayability_hooks: Five distinct game modes with different objectives
Multiple vehicle options with unique physics properties encourage relearning tracks
Time-attack mode for optimization and mastery

world_building_techniques: Anthropomorphic vehicle designs with visual personality (eyes as headlights)
Overhead passes and track infrastructure suggesting lived-in world
Gender differentiation in Japanese manual (Coupe identified as female, others male) adds characterization

faction_indicators: Vehicle selection reflects player identity/skill philosophy (F-Type vs. 4WD vs. Coupe)
Gender presentation of vehicles (Japanese manual distinction) creates character alignment options
danger_signals: Collision system—vehicle damage encourages careful play
Heavy vehicle weight creates consequences for poor cornering decisions
environmental_storytelling: Billboard advertisements in racing environments suggest commercial world context
Road infrastructure (overhead passes, bumps) implies established racing circuits
Diverse track design suggests different racing venues with distinct character

sources_used: wikipedia: Stunt Race FX article (gameplay, development, producer/director credits, vehicle physics design philosophy)
